hero_summary: Awj Iraq is a maqam of the Sikah family whose tonic is Sikah (E half-flat), and its defining trait is a descending sayr: it begins in the upper register and gradually descends until it settles on the tonic.
short_identity: A maqam of the Sikah family whose tonic is Sikah (E half-flat), and which in practical presentation is built from Jins Awj Iraq together with Jins Sikah inside a distinctive descending route.

quick_facts.ghammaz: The upper region where Jins Awj Iraq appears
quick_facts.character: A Sikah branch with a distinctive descending route and a clear melodic identity
quick_facts.common_start: It begins from the upper register and the jawab, then descends through Nawa (G), Jaharkah (F), and Dukah (D) until it settles on the Sikah tonic

sayr.summary: Awj Iraq is a maqam of the Sikah family whose tonic is Sikah (E half-flat), and it is distinguished by a descending sayr. In the practical form shown by the reference, the maqam is built from Jins Awj Iraq together with Jins Sikah. For that reason it should not be understood as ordinary Sikah, but as a Sikah branch with a distinctive descending path that begins in the upper register and gradually falls to the tonic.
resting_tones: Sikah (E half-flat): the final tonic of the maqam
The upper register where Jins Awj Iraq appears: the area that shapes the beginning and first emphasis of the maqam
Nawa (G), Jaharkah (F), and Dukah (D): essential passing tones in the descent toward the tonic
motion_notes: The maqam’s character is not understood through the scale alone, but through the descending path that gives it its special shape
It begins practically from the upper register, then descends through its essential tones until it settles on Sikah (E half-flat)
If the sense of descent is absent, the maqam may blur into other Sikah-family branches
If the Sikah tonic is not clearly affirmed at the end, the family identity weakens and the result becomes closer to a general melodic formula than to a specific maqam
common_path: Launch clearly from the upper jawab region
Bring out Jins Awj Iraq in the upper register
Descend through Nawa (G), Jaharkah (F), Sikah (E half-flat), and Dukah (D)
Return to the Sikah tonic with a firm cadence
common_modulations: Awj Iraq belongs to the Sikah family, but it is not ordinary Sikah, because its identity rests not only on the Sikah tonic but also on Jins Awj Iraq and the descending route
It is close to Huzam by family affiliation, but Huzam is defined more through a different upper coloration, whereas Awj Iraq is defined by beginning above and descending to the tonic
It is close to Iraq by name and modal world, yet in the practical form adopted here it is clearly presented as a Sikah-tonic maqam built from Jins Awj Iraq together with Jins Sikah
The auditory profile of Awj Iraq is not plain Sikah in the simple sense, but rather upper-register tension that gradually stabilizes below
